intro: Where the wine sometimes leads me...

poems.title: The Journey
poems.body:
I want to taste my way through the villages of Burgundy,
Tasting the earth from vineyards that have been around for eternity.
I want to relish in the tannins of Bordeaux,
Going from the left bank to the right from Château to Château.
I want to bite into a Barolo from Piedmont,
Slowly tasting the wines evolve as they slowly decant.
I want to bask in the sunny vineyards of Tuscany,
Pairing farm to table in every village drinking Chianti.
I want to find my self in a wine cave with a bold Cabernet in Napa Valley,
Making my way down to Yountville finishing the day off with a six course meal at the French Laundry.
I want to settle down in the rolling hills of Santa Barbara wine country,
Where I can grow old with my wife and have my children's children call me Grand Daddy.
